---
title: "Geocomputation with R"
subtitle: "Geographic raster data with terra"
author: "Jannes Muenchow, Robin Lovelace, Jakub Nowosad"
date: 2026-07-06
---

## Setup

Slides and code: <https://github.com/geocompx/user26>

```{r}
#| eval: false
install.packages(c("terra", "spData"))
```

```{r}
library(terra)
library(spData)
```

## The raster data model

::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="58%"}
::: {.incremental}
- Continuous fields represented by a grid of **cells** (pixels).
- One (or more) attribute value per cell.
- Suitable for continuous data without sharp borders (elevation, temperature,
  satellite imagery).
- Structure: a **header** (extent, resolution, nrow/ncol, CRS) plus a **matrix**
  of values.
:::
:::

::: {.column width="42%"}
![](/img/raster.png)
:::
:::

Further reading: <https://r.geocompx.org/spatial-class#raster-data>

## Raster data in R: the terra package

The modern raster package is **terra** (successor to `raster`, C++-based,
faster, larger-than-memory support). Let's build a raster from scratch:

```{r}
elev = rast(nrows = 6, ncols = 6,
            xmin = -1.5, xmax = 1.5, ymin = -1.5, ymax = 1.5,
            vals = 1:36)
```

## A SpatRaster object

```{r}
elev
```

## Plotting a raster

```{r}
#| fig-height: 4.5
plot(elev)
```

# Raster attribute operations {.section-divider}

## Attribute subsetting

A raster is a matrix, so we can subset with `[i, j]`:

```{r}
elev[1, 1]
elev[6, 6]
```

. . .

... or with a single cell number:

```{r}
elev[1]
```

Further reading: <https://r.geocompx.org/attr#raster-subsetting>

## Map algebra: local operations

Cell-by-cell ("local") operations. You can use:

- arithmetic operators: `+`, `-`, `*`, `/`
- logical operators: `>`, `>=`, `<`, `==`, `!`
- functions: `abs()`, `round()`, `sqrt()`, `log()`, `exp()`, ...

```{r}
#| eval: false
elev + 1
elev^2
elev / 4
```

The **NDVI** (from red and near-infrared bands) is a classic local operation.

## Map algebra: focal operations

A **focal** operation applies a function over a central cell **and its
neighbours** (a moving window / kernel, typically 3×3).

```{r}
r_focal = focal(elev, w = 3, fun = "min")
```

![](/img/04_focal_example.png){width="85%" fig-align="center"}

## Map algebra: zonal operations

**Zonal** operations summarise values within zones defined by a second raster.
Here: mean `elev` per soil-grain class.

```{r}
elev  = rast(system.file("raster/elev.tif",  package = "spData"))
grain = rast(system.file("raster/grain.tif", package = "spData"))
zonal(elev, grain, fun = "mean")
```

## Map algebra: global operations

**Global** operations treat the whole raster as a single zone — e.g. summary
statistics for the entire dataset.

```{r}
global(elev, fun = "mean")
global(elev, fun = "sd")
```

## Your turn

```{r}
# a small elevation raster of Luxembourg, shipped with terra
dem = rast(system.file("ex/elev.tif", package = "terra"))
```

1. Retrieve the elevation values of the 10th **row** of `dem`
   (hint: `dem[10, ]`).
2. Extract the `dem` values at 10 random coordinates
   (hint: `spatSample(dem, 10, xy = TRUE)`).
3. Compute the mean, min and max elevation of `dem` with `global()`.

# Geometric operations {.section-divider}

## Cropping and masking

Raster–vector interaction: restrict a raster to a study area.

```{r}
#| fig-height: 3.4
elev_lux = rast(system.file("ex/elev.tif", package = "terra"))
lux = vect(system.file("ex/lux.shp", package = "terra"))
diekirch = lux[lux$NAME_1 == "Diekirch", ]
elev_masked = mask(crop(elev_lux, diekirch), diekirch)
plot(elev_masked); plot(diekirch, add = TRUE)
```

## Extracting values at points

```{r}
pts = centroids(lux)                     # one point per district
pts$elev = extract(elev_lux, pts)[, 2]
head(pts$elev)
```

## Aggregation (changing resolution)

```{r}
#| fig-height: 3.4
elev_agg = aggregate(elev, fact = 2, fun = "mean")
par(mfrow = c(1, 2))
plot(elev, main = "original")
plot(elev_agg, main = "aggregated (fact = 2)")
par(mfrow = c(1, 1))
```

Use `disagg()` to increase resolution.

## Reprojecting a raster

Change the CRS of a raster with `project()` (EPSG codes are accepted):

```{r}
crs(elev_lux, describe = TRUE)$name
```

. . .

```{r}
elev_proj = project(elev_lux, "EPSG:3035")   # LAEA Europe
crs(elev_proj, describe = TRUE)$name
```

Further reading: <https://r.geocompx.org/geometry-operations#geo-ras>

## Your turn

1. Decrease the resolution of `dem` by a factor of 5 (`aggregate()`), and plot
   the result.
2. Reproject `dem` to LAEA Europe (EPSG:3035) and plot it next to the original.
3. Crop **and** mask `elev_lux` to the "Grevenmacher" district of `lux`. What is
   the difference between `crop()` and `mask()`?

## Recap

With `terra` `SpatRaster` objects we covered:

- **Attribute** operations (subsetting, map algebra: local/focal/zonal/global)
- **Geometric** operations (crop, mask, extract, aggregate, reproject)

Next: **visualization with `tmap`**.

## References {.smaller}

::: {#refs}
:::
